title: Hugging Face launches ML Intern, AI agent that beats Claude Code on reasoning
summary: Hugging Face launches ML Intern, AI agent that beats Claude Code on reasoning | ETIH EdTech News — EdTech Innovation Hub Hugging Face releases ML Intern, the AI agent teaching itself to beat Claude Code on scientific reasoning 23 Apr New open source tool autonomously reads papers, pulls da
content: Hugging Face launches ML Intern, AI agent that beats Claude Code on reasoning | ETIH EdTech News — EdTech Innovation Hub [...] Hugging Face releases ML Intern, the AI agent teaching itself to beat Claude Code on scientific reasoning [...] New open source tool autonomously reads papers, pulls datasets and runs GPU training jobs, with Hugging Face putting up $1,000 in compute and Anthropic credits for early users. [...] Hugging Face has released ML Intern, an open source AI agent that autonomously researches, writes and runs machine learning code, with early benchmark results showing it outperforming Anthropic's Claude Code on scientific reasoning and OpenAI's Codex on a healthcare evaluation. [...] Aksel Joonas Reedi, who works on AI agents at Hugging Face, announced the release on LinkedIn and said the agent pulls papers from arXiv and hf.co/papers, walks citation graphs, pulls and reformats datasets, and launches training jobs on Hugging Face Jobs when local GPUs are not available. Hugging Face is also provisioning $1,000 in GPU resources and Anthropic credits for early users. [...] ML Intern beats Claude Code and Codex on benchmarks [...] Reedi said on LinkedIn that ML Intern was set to train the best LLM for scientific reasoning and found NVIDIA research including OpenScience and NemoTron-CrossThink through citation searches, before running 12 supervised fine-tuning passes on Qwen3-1.7B: "This pushed the score → on GPQA in under 10h. Claude Code's best: 22.," he posted. [...] On a separate healthcare test, Reedi said the agent decided existing datasets were too low quality, wrote a script to generate 1,100 synthetic data points covering emergency, client and multilingual communication, then upsampled the data 50 times for training. He said it "Beat Codex on HealthBench by ." For a competitive mathematics task, he added that the agent wrote a full GRPO training script, launched it on A100 GPUs via Hugging Face Spaces, and ran ablations after initial rewards collapsed until it succeeded. [...] Reedi added [...] Hugging Face has provisioned $1,000 in GPU resources and Anthropic credits for "the quickest" early users of [...] tool, with the CLI and web app both live today. The incentive lands as universities, bootcamps and EdTech startups are under [...] to give students and staff hands-on [...] to model training without paying commercial cloud rates. [...] open source and built on its [...] limits in public.

title: Google Releases Deep Research Max, Tops HLE, BrowseComp & DeepSearchQA Benchmarks
summary: Google Releases Deep Research Max, Tops HLE, BrowseComp & DeepSearchQA Benchmarks The frontier labs are continuing to release ever-better AI models with predictable regularity. Google DeepMind’s latest move is the launch of Deep Research Max — a new autonomous research agent built on Gemini 3.1 Pr
content: Google Releases Deep Research Max, Tops HLE, BrowseComp & DeepSearchQA Benchmarks [...] Google DeepMind’s latest move is the launch of Deep Research Max — a new autonomous research agent built on Gemini 3.1 Pro that the company says represents a material step forward in long-horizon research quality, benchmark performance, and enterprise readiness. [...] Google is introducing two distinct configurations. Deep Research replaces the preview agent released in December 2025, delivering lower latency and reduced cost — designed for real-time, interactive surfaces. Deep Research Max, the headline release, is built for depth over speed. It leverages extended test-time compute to iteratively reason, search, and refine its outputs, making it best suited for asynchronous workflows: think a nightly cron job that produces exhaustive due diligence reports by morning. [...] Benchmark Numbers That Stand Out [...] On three industry-standard benchmarks, Deep Research Max posts results that outpace both its predecessor and competing models: [...] - DeepSearchQA (comprehensive web research): 93., versus 81.for the new Deep Research, 66.for the December release, and 88.for GPT 5.4 Thinking (xhigh) - Humanity’s Last Exam (reasoning and knowledge): 54., versus 50.for Deep Research, 46.for the December release, and 53.for GPT 5.4 - BrowseComp (locating hard-to-find facts): 85., versus 61.for Deep Research, 59.for the December release, and 58.for GPT 5.4 [...] The gains over the December 2025 version are significant across the board — not marginal. Google also beats Anthropic’s Opus 4.6 on all three benchmarks, though Gemini’s momentum in the broader AI market has been building for some time now. [...] the more consequential updates are functional. Deep [...] MCP (Model [...] ) servers, transforming it [...] proprietary and specialized data repositories [...] , custom document [...] generative AI market. Deep Research Max is not a [...] s a developer and enterprise offering available [...] s Interactions API on paid tiers. But it signals where [...] bets for enterprise AI: [...] Whether the benchmark numbers translate to production-grade utility at scale remains to be seen. But the architecture — blending open web search with proprietary data streams, native visualizations, and MCP extensibility — is a serious enterprise proposition, not just a benchmark play.

title: Google DeepMind launches Deep Research Max autonomous AI research agent
summary: Google DeepMind launches Deep Research Max autonomous AI research agent | ETIH EdTech News — EdTech Innovation Hub Google's new AI agent will run 160 searches while you sleep and hand you the report by morning 22 Apr New Gemini 3.1 Pro-powered agents hit public preview with MCP support, na
content: Google DeepMind launches Deep Research Max autonomous AI research agent | ETIH EdTech News — EdTech Innovation Hub [...] New Gemini 3.1 Pro-powered agents hit public preview with MCP support, native charts and multimodal inputs, as Google positions Deep Research as an enterprise workflow engine for finance and life sciences. [...] Google DeepMind has launched Gemini 3.1 Pro-powered Deep Research and Deep Research Max agents in public preview via the Gemini API. [...] Google DeepMind has launched two new autonomous research agents, Deep Research and Deep Research Max, in public preview via the Gemini API. Built on Gemini 3.1 Pro, the agents search the open web, user file uploads and connected data sources through Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers, generate charts and infographics natively, and consult over 100 sources in a single task. [...] Google is positioning the release as a move beyond summarization into an enterprise workflow layer for finance, life sciences and market research, with named financial data partners already building MCP integrations. [...] The standard Deep Research agent replaces the December preview release and is optimized for speed and lower cost, aimed at interactive user-facing products. Deep Research Max is the heavier option, designed for asynchronous background workflows such as overnight due diligence reports, and uses extended test-time compute to iteratively search and refine its output. [...] Max runs around 160 search queries per task, according to Philipp Schmid, AI Developer Experience at Google DeepMind, who announced the update on LinkedIn. The two new models are "deep-research-preview-04-2026" and "deep-research-max-preview-04-2026". Schmid reported benchmark scores of 93.3 percent on DeepSearchQA for web research and 85.9 percent on BrowseComp for hard-fact retrieval. [...] pull in user [...] Google's new AI agent will run 160 searches while you sleep and hand you the report by morning

title: OpenAI Announces ChatGPT Images 2.0
summary: OpenAI Announces ChatGPT Images 2.0 - Thurrott.com ChatGPT Images 2.0 is a more precise image generation capability for complex creative tasks of all kinds. “OpenAI is launching ChatGPT Images 2.0 today, a new image generation model available across ChatGPT, Codex, and the API,” an OpenAI represen
content: OpenAI Announces ChatGPT Images 2.0 - Thurrott.com ChatGPT Images 2.0 is a more precise image generation capability for complex creative tasks of all kinds. “OpenAI is launching ChatGPT Images 2.0 today, a new image generation model available across ChatGPT, Codex, and the API,” an OpenAI representative told me. “The update aims to make image generation more usable for real work, not just visual experimentation. Compared with prior versions, Images 2.0 is significantly better at following detailed instructions, rendering dense text, placing objects accurately, handling a wider range of aspect ratios, and generating images across more languages.” ChatGPT Images 2.0 is ideally suited for small text, UI elements, diagrams, and dense layouts, OpenAI says. It supports aspect ratios from 3:1 to 1:3, so you can create assets for banners, slides, posters, and mobile formats. There are thinking-enabled workflows so ChatGPT can reason through tasks, access the web if needed, and generate multiple images from a single prompt. And you can request up to 8 outputs from a single request for making multiple formats of an image all at once. In addition to its availability in ChatGPT and Codex, you can also access the underlying model, gpt-image-2, from the API so third-party developers can create their own solutions on top of these capabilities. You can learn more about ChatGPT Images 2.0 on the OpenAI website. Tagged with Share post

title: OpenAI Unveils New Image Generator to Usher in an AI Slop 'Renaissance'
summary: OpenAI Unveils New Image Generator to Usher in an AI Slop 'Renaissance' OpenAI Unveils New Image Generator to Usher in an AI Slop ‘Renaissance’ OpenAI wants you to forget about Nano Banana Pro and come back to ChatGPT, please.
content: OpenAI just unveiled a brand new image generator that it claims can churn out smarter and more precise slop than ever before. [...] ChatGPT Images 2.0 is going to be a “renaissance” in AI image generation, according to an introductory promo ad that ran before OpenAI’s livestream on Tuesday announcing the news. [...] “Images 2.0 is a huge step forward; this is like going from GPT-3 to GPT-5 all at once,” CEO Sam Altman said in the livestream. [...] The company boasts new multilingual capabilities, better visual intelligence, and closer attention to detail with the new model, showcasing a prompt that generated an image of a bowl of rice in which only a single tiny grain has the name of the model on it. [...] The model has two modes: instant and thinking. The researchers claimed both modes of the model are significantly better than previous image generation capabilities in ChatGPT, and that typos are “very rare.” [...] The instant mode is just a faster and revamped version of a typical image generator, it seems, and is available now to all ChatGPT and API users. The Thinking mode is more complex and only available to paid users, specifically subscribers to Plus, Pro, and Business. [...] “When a thinking model is selected in ChatGPT, Images 2.0 can search the web for real-time information, create multiple distinct images from one prompt, and double-check its own outputs,” OpenAI announced in a press release generated by Images 2.0 and made to look like a retro magazine spread. [...] If the new image generator model can capture the online success that the previous GPT-4o image generation grabbed with the “Studio Ghibli” craze a little over a year ago, it can help ChatGPT bump up its weekly active user numbers, another important point of consideration for investors. OpenAI announced in February that ChatGPT had breached 900 million weekly active users, and Images 2.0 could assist those numbers to reach the arbitrary but much more impressive-sounding 1 billion. [...] generator Nano Banana Pro and released [...] 3, both to significant fanfare. Promptly following the stellar reception of Google’s releases, OpenAI declared “ code red” at

title: OpenAI launches ChatGPT Images 2.0 with API access
summary: OpenAI launches ChatGPT Images 2.0 with API access IT Brief Canada - Technology news for CIOs & IT decision-makers OpenAI launches ChatGPT Images 2.0 with API access Tue, 21st Apr 2026 (Yesterday) By Sean Mitchell, Publisher OpenAI has launched ChatG
content: OpenAI launches ChatGPT Images 2.0 with API access [...] OpenAI launches ChatGPT Images 2.0 with API access [...] OpenAI has launched ChatGPT Images 2.0, making the image generation model available across ChatGPT, Codex and its API. [...] ChatGPT Images 2.0 introduces a new model, gpt-image-2, which developers can also access through the API to add image generation and editing to their own products and workflows. Access is rolling out to all ChatGPT and Codex users, while advanced outputs that use OpenAI's "Thinking" tools are reserved for Plus, Pro, Business and Enterprise users. [...] The latest version is designed to improve on earlier models in following detailed instructions, placing objects accurately and rendering dense text. It also supports a wider range of aspect ratios, from 3:1 to 1:3, to suit formats such as banners, slides, posters and mobile layouts. [...] The launch reflects a broader push across the AI industry to move image generation beyond novelty and into marketing, publishing, design and product workflows. OpenAI is positioning the model for tasks such as creating recipe pages, magazine covers, storyboards, manga layouts and social media assets in different formats from a single request. [...] Another addition is "Thinking"-enabled image workflows in ChatGPT. When users select a thinking or pro model, the system can reason through a task, use web information where relevant, generate several distinct images from one prompt and check its own output. OpenAI says this is aimed at tasks where consistency, current information and close adherence to instructions matter. [...] the update as a step change in instruction-following and in handling visual details that have often proved difficult for image models. These include small text, diagrams, iconography, user interface elements and dense layouts, where businesses and professional users have often found earlier tools limiting. [...] The model also works across more languages and can draw on broader visual and world knowledge to fill gaps in a prompt. In the API, images can be produced at up to 2K resolution. [...] The release also extends OpenAI's effort to tie visual generation more closely to its wider product range. By making the model available in ChatGPT, Codex and the API at the same time, it is offering access to both end users and developers that want to build image tools into software and business processes.
